---
title: "Experiment 1"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
# Packages
library(lme4)
library(stats)
library(purrr)
library(lmerTest)  # optional, for p-values in summaries

df_allsubjs_test <- read.csv(file.path("data","exp1_data.csv"))
# Only analyze trials where participants deliberately submitted a solution (as opposed to time-outs)
df_allsubjs_test <- df_allsubjs_test[df_allsubjs_test$"submitted_in_time" > 0, ]
df_allsubjs_test$cond <- as.numeric(df_allsubjs_test$cond)
```



```{r}
# ----- Metrics -----
log_loss <- function(y, p, eps = 1e-15) {
  p <- pmin(pmax(p, eps), 1 - eps)
  -mean(y * log(p) + (1 - y) * log(1 - p))
}

brier_score <- function(y, p) {
  mean((p - y)^2)
}

acc_at_0_5 <- function(y, p) {
  mean((p >= 0.5) == y)
}

# ----- Stratified grouped K-fold for binary response -----
# Holds out entire participants while trying to balance the binary response rate across folds.
make_stratified_folds <- function(y, group, K = 5, seed = 0) {
  set.seed(seed)

  y <- as.integer(y)
  group <- as.character(group)

  if (!all(y %in% c(0, 1))) {
    stop("y must be binary 0/1.")
  }

  group_levels <- unique(group)

  if (length(group_levels) < K) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Number of groups (%d) is smaller than K (%d).",
      length(group_levels), K
    ))
  }

  # Group-level summaries
  group_summary <- data.frame(
    group = group_levels,
    n = as.integer(tabulate(match(group, group_levels))),
    n_pos = as.integer(tapply(y, group, sum)[group_levels])
  )

  group_summary$prop_pos <- group_summary$n_pos / group_summary$n

  # Randomize ties, then sort by response proportion so high/low subjects are distributed
  group_summary <- group_summary[sample(seq_len(nrow(group_summary))), ]
  group_summary <- group_summary[order(-group_summary$prop_pos), ]

  folds_groups <- vector("list", K)
  fold_n <- rep(0, K)
  fold_pos <- rep(0, K)

  global_prop <- mean(y)

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(group_summary))) {
    this_n <- group_summary$n[i]
    this_pos <- group_summary$n_pos[i]

    # Greedily assign this participant to the fold that best preserves balance
    candidate_scores <- sapply(seq_len(K), function(k) {
      new_n <- fold_n[k] + this_n
      new_pos <- fold_pos[k] + this_pos
      new_prop <- new_pos / new_n

      # Balance response rate, with a tiny penalty for fold size imbalance
      abs(new_prop - global_prop) + 0.001 * new_n
    })

    k_best <- which.min(candidate_scores)

    folds_groups[[k_best]] <- c(folds_groups[[k_best]], group_summary$group[i])
    fold_n[k_best] <- fold_n[k_best] + this_n
    fold_pos[k_best] <- fold_pos[k_best] + this_pos
  }

  # Return row indices
  folds <- lapply(folds_groups, function(gs) {
    which(group %in% gs)
  })

  folds
}

# ----- CV wrapper for logistic GLMM: between-participant generalization -----
cv_glmm_kfold <- function(df, response, group, fixed_formula, random_spec,
                          K = 5, seed = 0,
                          optimizer = "bobyqa", nAGQ = 0) {
  # Sanity check for binary response
  uy <- sort(unique(df[[response]]))
  if (!all(uy %in% c(0, 1))) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "%s must be 0/1, got: %s",
      response,
      paste(uy, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }

  df[[group]] <- as.factor(df[[group]])

  # Build model formula
  fml <- as.formula(
    paste0(response, " ~ ", fixed_formula, " + ", random_spec)
  )

  # Fit on full data, for final summary / AIC / BIC
  fit_full <- suppressWarnings(
    glmer(
      fml,
      data = df,
      family = binomial(),
      control = glmerControl(optimizer = optimizer),
      nAGQ = nAGQ
    )
  )

  # Grouped folds: all trials from a participant go into the same fold
  folds <- make_stratified_folds(
    y = df[[response]],
    group = df[[group]],
    K = K,
    seed = seed
  )

  rows <- vector("list", K)

  for (k in seq_len(K)) {
    te_idx <- folds[[k]]
    tr_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(df)), te_idx)

    train <- droplevels(df[tr_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    test  <- df[te_idx, , drop = FALSE]

    # Keep held-out subject IDs as new levels
    test[[group]] <- factor(
      as.character(test[[group]]),
      levels = union(
        levels(train[[group]]),
        unique(as.character(test[[group]]))
      )
    )

    fit_k <- suppressWarnings(
      glmer(
        fml,
        data = train,
        family = binomial(),
        control = glmerControl(optimizer = optimizer),
        nAGQ = nAGQ
      )
    )

    # Between-participant prediction:
    # held-out subjects have no estimated random effects,
    # so use fixed effects only.
    p_test <- suppressWarnings(
      predict(
        fit_k,
        newdata = test,
        type = "response",
        re.form = NA,
        allow.new.levels = TRUE
      )
    )

    y_test <- test[[response]]

    rows[[k]] <- data.frame(
      fold = k,
      n_train = nrow(train),
      n_test = nrow(test),
      n_train_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(train[[group]]),
      n_test_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(test[[group]]),
      n_with_RE = 0,
      n_FE_only = nrow(test),
      mean_logloss = log_loss(y_test, p_test),
      brier = brier_score(y_test, p_test),
      acc = acc_at_0_5(y_test, p_test)
    )
  }

  out <- do.call(rbind, rows)

  avg <- colMeans(out[, c("mean_logloss", "brier", "acc")])

  list(
    cv_table = out,
    cv_avg = avg,
    fit_full = fit_full,
    cv_type = "between-participant grouped K-fold"
  )
}

```


```{r}
# ----- Metric for regression -----
mse <- function(y, yhat) mean((y - yhat)^2)

# ----- Grouped random K-fold for regression -----
# Holds out entire participants.
make_random_folds <- function(group, K = 5, seed = 0) {
  set.seed(seed)

  group <- as.character(group)
  group_levels <- unique(group)

  if (length(group_levels) < K) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Number of groups (%d) is smaller than K (%d).",
      length(group_levels), K
    ))
  }

  group_levels <- sample(group_levels)

  folds_groups <- split(
    group_levels,
    rep(seq_len(K), length.out = length(group_levels))
  )

  folds <- lapply(folds_groups, function(gs) {
    which(group %in% gs)
  })

  folds
}

# ----- CV wrapper for LME (test MSE; includes REs when available) -----
# ----- CV wrapper for LME: between-participant generalization -----
cv_lme_kfold <- function(df, response, group, fixed_formula, random_spec,
                         K = 5, seed = 0,
                         optimizer = "bobyqa") {
  if (!is.numeric(df[[response]])) {
    stop(sprintf("%s must be numeric for LME.", response))
  }

  df[[group]] <- as.factor(df[[group]])

  if ("cond" %in% names(df) && !is.factor(df$cond)) {
    df$cond <- as.factor(df$cond)
  }

  # Build model formula
  fml <- as.formula(
    paste0(response, " ~ ", fixed_formula, " + ", random_spec)
  )

  # Optional full-data fit
  fit_full <- suppressWarnings(
    lmer(
      fml,
      data = df,
      control = lmerControl(optimizer = optimizer),
      REML = FALSE
    )
  )

  # Grouped folds: all rows from a participant go into the same fold
  folds <- make_random_folds(
    group = df[[group]],
    K = K,
    seed = seed
  )

  rows <- vector("list", K)

  for (k in seq_len(K)) {
    te_idx <- folds[[k]]
    tr_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(df)), te_idx)

    train <- droplevels(df[tr_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    test  <- df[te_idx, , drop = FALSE]

    if ("cond" %in% names(train)) {
      test$cond <- factor(test$cond, levels = levels(train$cond))
    }

    # Keep held-out subject IDs as new levels
    test[[group]] <- factor(
      as.character(test[[group]]),
      levels = union(
        levels(train[[group]]),
        unique(as.character(test[[group]]))
      )
    )

    fit_k <- suppressWarnings(
      lmer(
        fml,
        data = train,
        control = lmerControl(optimizer = optimizer),
        REML = FALSE
      )
    )

    y_test <- test[[response]]

    # Between-participant prediction:
    # held-out participants have no random-effect estimates,
    # so use fixed effects only.
    yhat <- suppressWarnings(
      predict(
        fit_k,
        newdata = test,
        re.form = NA,
        allow.new.levels = TRUE
      )
    )

    rows[[k]] <- data.frame(
      fold = k,
      n_train = nrow(train),
      n_test = nrow(test),
      n_train_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(train[[group]]),
      n_test_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(test[[group]]),
      n_with_RE = 0,
      n_FE_only = nrow(test),
      test_mse = mse(y_test, yhat)
    )
  }

  out <- do.call(rbind, rows)

  list(
    cv_table = out,
    cv_avg_mse = mean(out$test_mse),
    fit_full = fit_full,
    cv_type = "between-participant grouped K-fold"
  )
}
```



# Below is Conclusion I)

```{r}
response <- "path_on_rim"   # 0/1
group    <- "Subj"          # subject id column
seed <- 0

fixed_formula00 <- "1"
random_spec00   <- "(1 | Subj)"
res00b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_allsubjs_test, response, group,
                      fixed_formula00, random_spec00, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula1  <- "cond"
random_spec1    <- "(1 + cond || Subj)"
res1b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_allsubjs_test, response, group,
                      fixed_formula1, random_spec1, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula0 <- "path_len_diff"
random_spec0   <- "(1+path_len_diff || Subj)"
res0b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_allsubjs_test, response, group,
                      fixed_formula0, random_spec0, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula2 <- "cond + path_len_diff"
random_spec2   <- "(1+cond+path_len_diff || Subj)"
res2b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_allsubjs_test, response, group,
                      fixed_formula2, random_spec2, K = 5, seed = seed)


fixed_formula3 <- "cond + block + trial + path_len_diff"
random_spec3   <- "(1+cond+path_len_diff+block+trial || Subj)"
res3b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_allsubjs_test, response, group,
                      fixed_formula3, random_spec3, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula4 <- "block + trial + path_len_diff"
random_spec4   <- "(1+path_len_diff+block+trial || Subj)"
res4b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_allsubjs_test, response, group,
                      fixed_formula4, random_spec4, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula5 <- "cond + block + trial + path_len_diff + first_cond"
random_spec5   <- "(1+cond+path_len_diff+block+trial + first_cond || Subj)"
res5b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_allsubjs_test, response, group,
                      fixed_formula5, random_spec5, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula6 <- "block + trial + path_len_diff + first_cond"
random_spec6   <- "(1+path_len_diff+block+trial + first_cond || Subj)"
res6b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_allsubjs_test, response, group,
                      fixed_formula6, random_spec6, K = 5, seed = seed)

```




```{r}
print(BIC(res00b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res1b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res0b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res2b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res3b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res4b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res5b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res6b$fit_full))
print(res00b$cv_avg)
print(res1b$cv_avg)
print(res0b$cv_avg)
print(res2b$cv_avg)
print(res3b$cv_avg)
print(res4b$cv_avg)
print(res5b$cv_avg)
print(res6b$cv_avg)
print(" ")
summary(res3b$fit_full)
print(" ")
summary(res1b$fit_full)

```




# Below is LME code for III)

```{r}
df <- subset(df_allsubjs_test, !is.na(submitted_path_avgrt))
df <- subset(df, cond == 1)

response <- "submitted_path_avgrt"
group    <- "Subj"
seed <- 0

run_and_report <- function(df, response, group, fixed_formula, random_predictors) {
  # Build an independent RE spec over (1 + predictors || group)
  # If random_predictors is character(0), keep only intercept RE: (1 || group)
  if (length(random_predictors) == 0) {
    random_spec <- paste0("(1 | ", group, ")")
  } else {
    re_rhs <- paste(c("1", random_predictors), collapse = " + ")
    random_spec <- paste0("(", re_rhs, " || ", group, ")")
  }

  res <- cv_lme_kfold(
    df            = df,
    response      = response,
    group         = group,
    fixed_formula = fixed_formula, 
    random_spec   = random_spec,    
    K             = 5,
    seed          = seed,
    optimizer     = "bobyqa"
  )

  cv_tab <- res$cv_table
  cv_avg <- res$cv_avg_mse
  fit    <- res$fit_full
  bicv   <- BIC(fit)

  print(cv_tab)
  cat("\nAverages across folds:\n")
  cat(sprintf("test_mse: %.4f\n\n", cv_avg))
  print(summary(fit))

  list(test_mse = cv_avg, BIC = bicv)
}

# ------------------ Model 3: all three predictors -------------------
fixed_3  <- "path_on_rim + submitted_path_avgturns + submitted_path_avgnextsqs"
rand_3   <- c("path_on_rim", "submitted_path_avgturns", "submitted_path_avgnextsqs")
res_3    <- run_and_report(df, response, group, fixed_3, rand_3)
bic_value3 <- res_3$BIC
cv_avg3    <- res_3$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 21: path_on_rim + #turns -------------------
fixed_21 <- "path_on_rim + submitted_path_avgturns"
rand_21  <- c("path_on_rim", "submitted_path_avgturns")
res_21   <- run_and_report(df, response, group, fixed_21, rand_21)
bic_value21 <- res_21$BIC
cv_avg21    <- res_21$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 22: path_on_rim + #nextsqs -----------------
fixed_22 <- "path_on_rim + submitted_path_avgnextsqs"
rand_22  <- c("path_on_rim", "submitted_path_avgnextsqs")
res_22   <- run_and_report(df, response, group, fixed_22, rand_22)
bic_value22 <- res_22$BIC
cv_avg22    <- res_22$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 1: only path_on_rim ------------------------
fixed_1 <- "path_on_rim"
rand_1  <- c("path_on_rim")
res_1   <- run_and_report(df, response, group, fixed_1, rand_1)
bic_value1 <- res_1$BIC
cv_avg1    <- res_1$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 000: #1 ----------------------
fixed_000 <- "1"
rand_000  <- character(0)
res_000   <- run_and_report(df, response, group, fixed_000, rand_000)
bic_value000 <- res_000$BIC
cv_avg000    <- res_000$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 00: #turns + #nextsqs ----------------------
fixed_00 <- "submitted_path_avgturns + submitted_path_avgnextsqs"
rand_00  <- c("submitted_path_avgturns", "submitted_path_avgnextsqs")
res_00   <- run_and_report(df, response, group, fixed_00, rand_00)
bic_value00 <- res_00$BIC
cv_avg00    <- res_00$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 01: only #turns ----------------------------
fixed_01 <- "submitted_path_avgturns"
rand_01  <- c("submitted_path_avgturns")
res_01   <- run_and_report(df, response, group, fixed_01, rand_01)
bic_value01 <- res_01$BIC
cv_avg01    <- res_01$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 02: only #avgnextsqs -----------------------
fixed_02 <- "submitted_path_avgnextsqs"
rand_02  <- c("submitted_path_avgnextsqs")
res_02   <- run_and_report(df, response, group, fixed_02, rand_02)
bic_value02 <- res_02$BIC
cv_avg02    <- res_02$test_mse


# ------------------ Compare BIC and CV MSE ---------------------------
cat(sprintf("M3  BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value3,  cv_avg3))
cat(sprintf("M21 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value21, cv_avg21))
cat(sprintf("M22 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value22, cv_avg22))
cat(sprintf("M1  BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value1,  cv_avg1))
cat(sprintf("M01 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value01, cv_avg01))
cat(sprintf("M02 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value02, cv_avg02))
cat(sprintf("M00 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value00, cv_avg00))
cat(sprintf("M000 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value000, cv_avg000))

```


## Add additional block and trial order effects?

```{r}
# ------------------ Data filter ------------------
df <- subset(df_allsubjs_test, !is.na(submitted_path_avgrt))
df <- subset(df, cond == 1)

# Ensure types play nicely
df$Subj  <- as.factor(df$Subj)
if ("block" %in% names(df) && !is.factor(df$block)) {
  df$block <- as.factor(df$block)
}

response <- "submitted_path_avgrt"
group    <- "Subj"
seed <- 0

# Helper to run models, now always adding + block + trial as fixed effects,
# and adding independent random slopes for block and trial.
run_and_report_bt <- function(df, response, group, base_fixed, base_random_preds) {
  # Append fixed effects
  fixed_formula <- paste(base_fixed, "+ block + trial")

  # Random effects predictors: base + block + trial
  # Remove "1" if accidentally supplied
  rand_preds <- unique(c(base_random_preds, "block", "trial"))
  rand_preds <- setdiff(rand_preds, "1")

  # Random-effects spec
  # If there are slopes: independent intercept + slopes via ||
  # If no slopes: intercept-only via |
  if (length(rand_preds) == 0) {
    random_spec <- paste0("(1 | ", group, ")")
  } else {
    re_rhs <- paste(c("1", rand_preds), collapse = " + ")
    random_spec <- paste0("(", re_rhs, " || ", group, ")")
  }

  res <- cv_lme_kfold(
    df            = df,
    response      = response,
    group         = group,
    fixed_formula = fixed_formula, 
    random_spec   = random_spec,
    K             = 5,
    seed          = seed,
    optimizer     = "bobyqa"
  )

  cv_tab <- res$cv_table
  cv_avg <- res$cv_avg_mse
  fit    <- res$fit_full
  bicv   <- BIC(fit)

  print(cv_tab)
  cat("\nAverages across folds:\n")
  cat(sprintf("test_mse: %.4f\n\n", cv_avg))
  print(summary(fit))

  list(test_mse = cv_avg, BIC = bicv, fit_full=fit)
}

# ------------------ Model 3: all three predictors (+ block + trial) -------------------
fixed_3  <- "path_on_rim + submitted_path_avgturns + submitted_path_avgnextsqs"
rand_3   <- c("path_on_rim", "submitted_path_avgturns", "submitted_path_avgnextsqs")
res_3b    <- run_and_report_bt(df, response, group, fixed_3, rand_3)
bic_value3b <- res_3b$BIC
cv_avg3b    <- res_3b$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 21: path_on_rim + #turns (+ block + trial) -----------------
fixed_21 <- "path_on_rim + submitted_path_avgturns"
rand_21  <- c("path_on_rim", "submitted_path_avgturns")
res_21b   <- run_and_report_bt(df, response, group, fixed_21, rand_21)
bic_value21b <- res_21b$BIC
cv_avg21b    <- res_21b$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 22: path_on_rim + #nextsqs (+ block + trial) ---------------
fixed_22 <- "path_on_rim + submitted_path_avgnextsqs"
rand_22  <- c("path_on_rim", "submitted_path_avgnextsqs")
res_22b   <- run_and_report_bt(df, response, group, fixed_22, rand_22)
bic_value22b <- res_22b$BIC
cv_avg22b    <- res_22b$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 1: only path_on_rim (+ block + trial) ----------------------
fixed_1 <- "path_on_rim"
rand_1  <- c("path_on_rim")
res_1b   <- run_and_report_bt(df, response, group, fixed_1, rand_1)
bic_value1b <- res_1b$BIC
cv_avg1b    <- res_1b$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 000: (+ block + trial) ----------------------
fixed_000 <- "1"
rand_000  <- character(0)
res_000b   <- run_and_report_bt(df, response, group, fixed_000, rand_000)
bic_value000b <- res_000b$BIC
cv_avg000b    <- res_000b$test_mse


# ------------------ Model 00: #turns + #nextsqs (+ block + trial) --------------------
fixed_00 <- "submitted_path_avgturns + submitted_path_avgnextsqs"
rand_00  <- c("submitted_path_avgturns", "submitted_path_avgnextsqs")
res_00b   <- run_and_report_bt(df, response, group, fixed_00, rand_00)
bic_value00b <- res_00b$BIC
cv_avg00b    <- res_00b$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 01: only #turns (+ block + trial) --------------------------
fixed_01 <- "submitted_path_avgturns"
rand_01  <- c("submitted_path_avgturns")
res_01b   <- run_and_report_bt(df, response, group, fixed_01, rand_01)
bic_value01b <- res_01b$BIC
cv_avg01b    <- res_01b$test_mse

# ------------------ Model 02: only #avgnextsqs (+ block + trial) ---------------------
fixed_02 <- "submitted_path_avgnextsqs"
rand_02  <- c("submitted_path_avgnextsqs")
res_02b   <- run_and_report_bt(df, response, group, fixed_02, rand_02)
bic_value02b <- res_02b$BIC
cv_avg02b    <- res_02b$test_mse

```

```{r}
# ------------------ Compare BIC and CV MSE -------------------------------------------
cat(sprintf("M3  BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value3b,  cv_avg3b))
cat(sprintf("M21 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value21b, cv_avg21b))
cat(sprintf("M22 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value22b, cv_avg22b))
cat(sprintf("M1  BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value1b,  cv_avg1b))
cat(sprintf("M01 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value01b, cv_avg01b))
cat(sprintf("M02 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value02b, cv_avg02b))
cat(sprintf("M00 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value00b, cv_avg00b))
cat(sprintf("M000 BIC: %.2f, test_mse: %.6f\n",  bic_value000b, cv_avg000b))
```
